title: Review & setlist: Diana Krall brought a special kind of magic to The Cabot
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: Diana Krall performed at The Cabot in Beverly on March 28, 2026, showcasing her piano and vocal talents.
Krall was accompanied by bassist John Clayton, performing an 85-minute set with songs like 'Almost Like Being In Love.'
The concert included covers of classic songs by artists such as Frank Sinatra and Paul McCartney, ending with 'Come Dance With Me.'

title: Chelsea teen charged with murder after allegedly chasing down, stabbing man
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Sergio Josue Castellanos Cerrato, 18, charged with murder after allegedly stabbing Eduardo Alfredo Rosales Lopez in Chelsea.
Incident occurred near Chestnut Street and Everett Avenue on March 19; Lopez suffered life-threatening injuries and died.
Castellanos Cerrato and group reportedly pursued Lopez; he was allegedly seen stabbing Lopez in city camera footage.

title: 2025 marked Boston area’s slowest year of post-pandemic population growth, data shows
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The Boston metro area experienced its slowest population growth in 2025 since the COVID-19 pandemic recovery began.
Massachusetts' population grew by only 15,524 in 2025, marking the smallest statewide increase since 2021.
Middlesex and Worcester counties saw the largest increases, but growth was significantly lower than in 2024.

title: North Atlantic right whales now have a GoFundMe
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Cape Cod researchers launched a GoFundMe to support the conservation of North Atlantic right whales, raising $630 of a $15,000 goal.
The campaign funds projects like aerial and vessel surveys and equipment such as drones and marine binoculars for whale protection.
North Atlantic right whales face extinction from vessel strikes and entanglement, with recent deaths of young whales highlighting these risks.

title: 5 times the Patriots traded for wide receivers in recent history, and how each acquisition panned out
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Randy Moss joined the Patriots in 2007 from the Raiders, setting an NFL record with 23 touchdowns in a single season.
Wes Welker, acquired in 2007 from the Dolphins, became a prolific slot receiver, complementing Randy Moss effectively.
Josh Gordon joined the Patriots in 2018 but off-field issues limited his impact despite showing potential with over 700 yards in 11 games.

title: Alex Cora not harping on late-game ejection by C.B. Bucknor: ‘We’re all human’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora was ejected during the Boston Red Sox's 6-5 loss to the Cincinnati Reds after arguing a check swing call.
Red Sox shortstop Trevor Story and manager Cora were upset with umpire C.B. Bucknor's decision, but Cora later emphasized human error.
Roman Anthony criticized his decision to use Boston's final ABS challenge early in the game, leaving teammates without recourse later.

title: Trevor Story reacts to strike three call that got Alex Cora ejected in Red Sox’ loss
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Trevor Story disagreed with umpire CB Bucknor's strike three call during the Red Sox game against the opposition.
Manager Alex Cora was ejected after arguing with Bucknor over the disputed call late in the game.
Red Sox lost to the opponents 6-5 in 11 innings after tying the game in the ninth with a homer.

title: Brimming with motivation after missing Olympics, Pavel Zacha is playing his best hockey for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Pavel Zacha scored 11 goals and 19 points in 16 games after the Bruins' return from the Olympic break.
Missing the Olympics due to a concussion, Zacha has focused on becoming a difference maker for the Bruins.
Zacha, acquired from the Devils, has set a career-high in goals with 26 this season and aims to surpass his points record.

title: Here’s what Red Sox SP Sonny Gray said about his rough debut
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Sonny Gray allowed four runs in four innings during his Red Sox debut, resulting in a no-decision.
Gray faced his former team, the Cincinnati Reds, in Boston's 6-5, 11-inning loss on March 28, 2026.
Manager Alex Cora noted Gray's adjustments post-first inning, while Gray credited the bullpen for keeping the team in the game.

title: Tens of thousands rally at Boston Common for third ‘No Kings’ protest
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 150,000 people attended the 'No Kings' rally at Boston Common, part of a nationwide protest.
The event featured speeches by Governor Maura Healey and Senator Elizabeth Warren, focusing on democratic values.
The rally included performances by the Dropkick Murphys and opportunities to join advocacy groups.
